friction_items.description: tool_audit(scope=synthesis, dimension=all) on a humanities essay scaffolded with venue_template='humanities_essay' STILL reports missing_sections: [abstract, methods, results, discussion] and short_sections: [abstract, introduction, methods, results, discussion] with min_word_bar requiring 'Methods >= 400 words' and 'Results >= 400 words'. The blocker text reads 'Methods is 0 words - min 400. Pull more from workspace/<step>/step_summary.yaml and the per-step `## Findings` blocks.' For a literary essay there is no methods section and there are no per-step findings blocks. Phase 9 added the venue_template + scope_tags.workflow_shape:linear_essay metadata, but the audit gate code never reads it. The researcher hits a hard wall at synthesis time: tool_synthesize refuses with `BLOCKED by unresolved audit findings ledger (5 BLOCK finding(s))` until the user explicitly passes `override_unresolved_blocks=true AND override_rationale='<one-line why>'` — surfacing the override is an improvement vs baseline opaque 'gate fired', but the existence of the wrong-shape gate at all is still HIGH-severity friction.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Make the synthesis audit consult `venue_template` (or scope_tags.workflow_shape) and switch the section-shape expectation set. For 'humanities_essay': expected = [introduction, contextual_framing, close_reading_one, close_reading_two, close_reading_three, critical_conversation, counter_argument_and_reply, conclusion_and_stakes] with per-section word minimums tuned for prose essays. Drop figures_referenced/citation_density_per_1000_words STEM-style gates entirely for linear_essay shape; replace with anchor_density (interpretive claims with line/folio locators) + tradition_declared signal.

friction_items.description: close_reading.yaml has `scope_tags: { domain: [humanities, theory_math], audience: [auditor], workflow_shape: [proof] }`. Three problems in one tag block: (1) `audience: [auditor]` means a researcher-facing tool_route surface that filters on audience would HIDE the protocol from the researcher who needs it most; (2) `workflow_shape: [proof]` is wrong — a close reading is an apparatus + interpretive prose, not a formal proof; (3) `domain: [humanities, theory_math]` over-claims theory_math. Same audience=[auditor] mistake appears on citation_chains.yaml and source_provenance.yaml. Other humanities protocols (archival_research, hermeneutic_method) correctly tag audience=[researcher]. The metadata schema landed, but values were authored inconsistently.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Lint scope_tags at protocol-load time: a humanities/textual/* file with audience: [auditor] should fail validation. Standardize on audience: [researcher] for all 'how do I do X' protocols and audience: [auditor] only for the 6 audit/* + provenance/* protocols. Fix workflow_shape: close_reading -> apparatus_and_essay or just essay; the proof shape belongs only to theory_math/* lean/coq protocols. Add a CI test that walks all 118 protocols and asserts each audience tag matches the protocol's directory family.

friction_items.description: 146 live tools, of which 7 are step/path/branch lifecycle verbs: tool_path_finalize, sys_path, tool_step, tool_step_complete, tool_step_pipeline, tool_promote_to_step, tool_alternative_path_propose, tool_step_literature_list, tool_branch_recommendation. The path/step/branch model is RNAseq-pipeline shaped. A humanities researcher writing a single essay maps onto 1 path / 1 step / 0 branches — but the surface still presents the full pipeline ontology, and the audit blockers reference `workspace/<step>/step_summary.yaml` and `## Findings` blocks that a linear essay does not have. Phase 15a flagged researcher_config.workflow_shape: 'linear_essay' as a fix — the protocol-side scope_tags.workflow_shape: [linear_essay] now exists on humanities_essay_structure, but the TOOL surface still does not filter on it, and the AUDIT gates still demand step_summary.yaml regardless.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Plumb scope_tags.workflow_shape from the routed protocol into a session-level workflow_shape state that (a) tells tool_audit which gate-set to apply, (b) tells sys_active_tools / tool_route to omit tool_step_pipeline / tool_branch_recommendation / tool_alternative_path_propose for linear_essay sessions, and (c) lets sys_workspace_scaffold lay out workspace/draft/ + workspace/close_readings/ instead of workspace/01_..., workspace/02_....

friction_items.description: Six discovery tools still exist post-Phase-9: tool_route, tool_quick_route (new), tool_semantic_route, sys_semantic_tool_search, tool_protocols_list, tool_tools_list (plus sys_protocol_list for a 7th). Phase 15a flagged six and recommended collapsing to two. Phase 9 net added one (tool_quick_route) without removing any. Each has overlapping prose in its description. A researcher conversation only ever triggers tool_route; the other 5 generate token weight without ever firing.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Mark tool_quick_route + tool_semantic_route + sys_semantic_tool_search + tool_protocols_list + tool_tools_list as @internal so they vanish from list_tools when researcher_config.audience != 'maintainer'. Keep handlers for tests + maintainer debugging. Phase 9 added `status: live|alias|deprecated`; add an `internal` status (or surface filter) and apply it here.

friction_items.description: close_reading step 1 is still named 'Declare the step contract (close_reading IS an apparatus step)' in summary format. The vocabulary 'step contract' / 'apparatus step' is RO-internal — a humanist seeing this as the first action under a 'close reading' protocol thinks 'this is engineering software, not for me.' Phase 11b consolidated body prose but did not rename the apparatus-contract step or auto-side-effect the step_summary.yaml. Phase 15a explicitly flagged this; the fix did not land.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Rename step 1 to 'Set up your reading' and side-effect the step_summary.yaml writing inside tool_humanities_essay_scaffold so the researcher never sees the file. Keep the audit-side step_summary.yaml requirement; just remove it from the researcher-visible protocol body.

friction_items.description: tool_audit's first-call experience: a researcher passes `{'kind': 'synthesis'}` (intuitive arg name) and gets `tool_audit requires scope= and dimension=`. They then guess dimension='quality', 'completeness', 'paper', 'full' — all rejected with `unknown (scope='synthesis', dimension='X')`. Only dimension='all' works (or one of the four documented in the description). The description does list valid dimensions per scope, but error messages do not echo them when the user supplies a wrong one — the user has to scroll back to the tool description. Phase 9 unified 26 audit tools into 1, which is a major win; the per-dimension discovery is the last-mile gap.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Echo the valid dimensions list in the error when dimension is unknown: `tool_audit: unknown dimension='quality' for scope='synthesis'. Valid: all, dashboard_content, figure_coverage, reviewer_responses.` And accept the alias `dimension=full` as an alias for `dimension=all`.

friction_items.description: tool_route on 'I want to close-read Conrad Heart of Darkness and write a humanities essay' picks synthesis/humanities_essay_structure as primary (correct) and surfaces humanities/textual/close_reading at score 0.589 under `alternatives`. The decomposition is a sensible 4-tool chain (tool_humanities_essay_scaffold -> tool_synthesize_plan -> tool_synthesize -> tool_paper_compile_typst) but does NOT include close_reading as a prerequisite step — even though the user explicitly said 'close-read'. Phase 15a flagged `next_in_chain: synthesis/humanities_essay_structure` on close_reading; the symmetric `prerequisites_chain: [humanities/textual/close_reading]` on humanities_essay_structure is also missing. Net: researcher writes the essay shell before doing the close readings the essay sections need to draw from.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add `prerequisites_chain: [humanities/textual/close_reading]` to synthesis/humanities_essay_structure router entry. tool_route should then return a 2-protocol sequenced plan: 'first close_reading on each passage, THEN scaffold the essay, THEN draft sections from the close-reading apparatus files.' Update the decomposition output to include the close_reading step explicitly.

friction_items.description: tool_route accepts `prompt` but a researcher's instinct (and the description hints) is to call it with `query`. The error message `'prompt'` (a bare KeyError surfacing) is unfriendly. Phase 14 cleanup did not normalize this. Minor friction, easy fix.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Accept both `prompt` and `query` (alias). Replace the KeyError with `tool_route requires prompt= (the natural-language ask).`

friction_items.description: sys_protocol_get's parameter is `protocol_name`, not `name`. Calling with `name='humanities/textual/close_reading'` errors with `argument of type 'NoneType' is not iterable` — opaque NoneType crash, no hint that `protocol_name=` is required. Phase 9 changed the default format from 'full' to 'summary' (excellent), but the param-naming friction layered on top of an opaque error wastes a turn the first time a researcher's AI tries it.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Accept `name` as alias for `protocol_name`. Wrap missing-arg detection so the error reads `sys_protocol_get requires protocol_name=` rather than NoneType.

friction_items.description: Phase 15a recommended standardizing quality_bar to prose form. humanities_essay_structure.yaml's quality_bar is still a flat boolean dict in the loaded structure. close_reading.yaml uses the prose form (improvement landed there). Inconsistency between the two protocols in the same chain remains.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Convert humanities_essay_structure quality_bar from booleans to ~5 prose bars (thesis_falsifiability, close_reading_count_minimum_3, critical_conversation_named_critics, counter_argument_steelmanned, conclusion_stakes_beyond_text).

convoluted_tools: tool_route + tool_quick_route + tool_semantic_route + sys_semantic_tool_search + tool_protocols_list + tool_tools_list + sys_protocol_list (seven discovery tools, overlapping — Phase 9 added tool_quick_route without removing any of the six baseline ones)
tool_audit (unified, scope+dimension combinatorial; major win vs 26 baseline audit tools, but valid dimensions are not echoed in error messages)

tool_path_finalize + tool_step + tool_step_complete + tool_step_pipeline + tool_promote_to_step + tool_alternative_path_propose + tool_step_literature_list + tool_branch_recommendation (8 step/path/branch lifecycle verbs; collapsed to fewer top-level names but the ontology is still wrong-shape for a linear essay)
tool_plan + tool_plan_step + tool_plan_step_grounded + tool_plan_next_step (4 planning verbs)
tool_synthesize + tool_synthesize_plan + tool_synthesis_curate_figures + tool_synthesis_preview (4 synthesis verbs; the boundary between them is unclear to a researcher — when do I call which?)
confusing_protocols: humanities/textual/close_reading — scope_tags.audience=[auditor] and workflow_shape=[proof] are both wrong; step 1 still named 'Declare the step contract (close_reading IS an apparatus step)' using RO-internal vocabulary
synthesis/humanities_essay_structure — quality_bar still a boolean dict (not prose), prerequisites_chain to close_reading missing, audit-gate IMRAD mismatch (see HIGH friction items)
humanities/citation/citation_chains — still tagged audience=[auditor] (should be researcher); canonical-thinker jargon density unchanged
improvements_vs_baseline: Tool surface dropped from ~344 (baseline) to 146 live top-level defs — ~57% reduction. All carry the new `status: live` field, ready for an `alias|deprecated` lifecycle.

Per-tool `pack: core|humanities|qualitative|theory_math|wet_lab|engineering|slurm|snakemake|nextflow|cytoscape|redcap|synapse` metadata is present on every def — enables future pack-level filtering even though session-level filtering hasn't shipped yet.

tool_route output now includes `recommended_action`, `decomposition` (sequenced multi-tool plan), `why_matched` (Phase 11a, still present), `active_tools` shortlist (~17 tools, not 344), and `active_plan_path` persisted to .os_state/active_plan.json. Decomposition is the single biggest researcher-experience win — the AI now knows what to call next.
sys_protocol_get default format changed from 'full' to 'summary' (Phase 9 v2 breaking change landed). Token cost per protocol fetch dropped from ~1.5-3K to ~600-800 for a typical query.
tool_audit consolidated 26 per-dimension audit tools into one (`scope` + `dimension`). Description lists valid dimensions per scope inline. Major surface-area reduction.

Audit blockers now include actionable suggested_fix prose ('Methods is 0 words — min 400. Pull more from workspace/<step>/step_summary.yaml') and the synthesis-side block now tells the user the exact override args (`override_unresolved_blocks=true AND override_rationale='<one-line why>'`). Baseline's opaque 'gate fired' / 'stack_plan absent' strings are gone.

tool_humanities_essay_scaffold upgraded from a stub-headings shortcut to a full 8-section paper.md template with prose stage-directions per section + a `next_step` field pointing the AI at the thesis lock + per-turn drafting cadence. Baseline complained 'shortcut writes only stub headings'; that complaint is resolved.

scope_tags schema landed on every protocol with {domain, audience, workflow_shape}. The infrastructure exists for pack/role-based filtering even though the values are inconsistently authored (see HIGH friction item #2).
MCP `instructions` field naming the boot sequence is present, giving fresh clients a clear sys_boot -> tool_route -> sys_protocol_get -> tool_step entry pattern.

Modular server.py refactor (Phase 10) — handlers split into meta_routing / meta_workspace / meta_sys / methodology / research_search / research_exec / audit_core / audit_gates / synthesis_writing / synthesis_visual / synthesis_reviewer / grounding. A maintainer (and a deep-dive AI) can now find the right handler in seconds vs grepping a 7499-line monolith.
remaining_recommendations: Make tool_audit dispatch consult `venue_template` / scope_tags.workflow_shape and ship a humanities essay gate-set (sections: introduction/contextual_framing/close_readings/critical_conversation/counter_argument/conclusion_and_stakes; signals: anchor_density, tradition_declared, counter_instance; drop figures/IMRAD/citation-density). This is the single highest-impact remaining fix — without it the humanities researcher hits a wall the first time they try to compile.

Lint-fix scope_tags across all 118 protocols. close_reading + citation_chains + source_provenance audience tags + close_reading workflow_shape need correction. Add a CI test asserting each protocol's audience and workflow_shape are consistent with its directory family.

Plumb scope_tags.workflow_shape into a session-level state so sys_active_tools / tool_route hide tool_step_pipeline / tool_branch_recommendation / tool_alternative_path_propose for linear_essay sessions, and so sys_workspace_scaffold lays out workspace/draft/ + workspace/close_readings/ instead of the STEM workspace/01_step_a/ shape.

Add an `internal` status (or `audience: maintainer` filter) and apply it to tool_quick_route + tool_semantic_route + sys_semantic_tool_search + tool_protocols_list + tool_tools_list so they vanish from list_tools for non-maintainer sessions. tool_route + sys_protocol_list cover 99% of researcher discovery needs.

Add `prerequisites_chain: [humanities/textual/close_reading]` to synthesis/humanities_essay_structure router entry so tool_route returns a 2-protocol sequenced plan (close_reading -> essay scaffold -> draft -> compile).
Convert humanities_essay_structure quality_bar from booleans to prose form with measurable signals. Standardize prose quality_bar across all 118 protocols (only ~half migrated so far).
Echo valid dimensions in tool_audit error messages when an unknown dimension is supplied — currently the user has to scroll back to the description.

Accept aliases for the two most-common param-name mismatches: tool_route(`query` -> `prompt`) and sys_protocol_get(`name` -> `protocol_name`). Replace bare KeyError / NoneType crashes with explicit missing-arg errors.

Rename close_reading step 1 from 'Declare the step contract (close_reading IS an apparatus step)' to 'Set up your reading' and side-effect the step_summary.yaml write inside tool_humanities_essay_scaffold so the researcher never sees the RO-internal apparatus-contract vocabulary.
